title: We Can Come Back to Each Other
subtitle: An affirmation for the drift in a long relationship
excerpt: A short affirmation for partners who have grown quiet with each other without quite noticing until they did. Holds the possibility of reconnection without requiring that it be immediate or dramatic.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education, drawing on attachment theory and the relational tradition in couples work.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you notice the distance between you and your partner and it feels permanent rather than recoverable.
practice.whenNotToUse: Skip if the relationship is in active crisis or safety is a concern.

content.text: A short affirmation for the long relationship that has gone quiet. The drift is ordinary. It happens slowly and often without drama. The affirmation holds the door open for the return without requiring it to happen now.
Say it quietly, to yourself, in whatever room you are in. Say it three times.
We can come back to each other.

The affirmation is most useful before a conversation, or after a quiet evening that felt more separate than it should have. It is not a promise that you will. It is a statement that the possibility remains.

Written for homemade.education, drawing on attachment theory's understanding that secure relationships can tolerate distance and return from it. The repair is possible. The reaching toward it is the work.
